repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection consists of historical documents relating to Ronald Furry's 42 years on the Cornell faculty in the teaching, research and Extension programs, including course material, research material and items on the Layer Fund.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Following 42 years of service at Cornell University, Ronald Bay Furry (b. October 22, 1931), Professor of Agricultural and Biological Engineering, retired August 30, 1995 and was granted Emeritus status. Furry received his BS (1953, with Distinction) and MS (1955) degrees from Cornell University, and his PhD (1965) from Iowa State University.

During his tenure at Cornell, Furry participated in all three mission functions of the Department: Teaching, Research and Extension, and served as its Chairman during 1990-94. His principal research interests centered on postharvest controlled atmosphere environments for agricultural materials, while his Extension efforts included responsibility for the dairy structures program for New York State, as well as the Extension Plan Service. He enjoyed teaching immensely, and taught undergraduate and graduate courses ranging from computer programming and computer graphics through similitude methodology. His publications include fifteen sole-author, eighteen principal-author, and seventeen contributing-author papers; sixty engineering bulletins, mostly sole-authored; four manuals and guides; one book chapter; one-hundred-seventy articles; thirty-two structural design plan sets; and seventy-eight serial newsletters. In addition, he developed several mainframe and microcomputer engineering design application packages which he utilized extensively in his teaching program. He also served the Department as Coordinator of Research and Graduate Faculty Representative, participated in a wide variety of College of Agriculture and Life Sciences, College of Engineering and University responsibilities, including the University Senate and the Faculty Council of Representatives, and established and conducted the Department's undergraduate recruitment program from 1974 to his retirement. In 2007, he wrote A Pioneering Department: Evolution from Rural Engineering to Biological and Environmental Engineering at Cornell University, 1907-2007, that traces the evolution of the Department of Biological and Environmental Engineering (BEE) at Cornell over its first 100 years.

Furry received early recognition as a National Science Foundation Science Faculty Fellow, and a Ford Foundation Prospective Teacher in Engineering. In 1992, he was elected a Fellow of the American Society of Agricultural Engineers (ASAE, now ASABE). He was the recipient of several blue ribbon awards from ASAE. In 1993, the American Society for Engineering Education (ASEE) awarded him its Centennial Certificate for Exceptional Contributions to ASEE and the Profession of Engineering. He also served as Chairman of the Biological and Agricultural Engineering Division of ASEE.

Furry is a member of Phi Kappa Phi, Sigma Xi, a charter member of Gamma Sigma Delta at Cornell, and a member of Alpha Zeta (AZ), where he served on the Board of Directors for several decades; he was the AZ Board's Facility Program Chairman during the construction of the fraternity's new house which was dedicated in 1993. He was a charter member (1962) of the Ithaca Sertoma Club, and an early member (1954) and active participant of the Cornell Federal Credit Union (CFCU), now CFCU Community Credit Union. He served on the Credit Union's Board of Directors for eleven years, and was its Chairman for four years. He was a Distinguished Military Graduate of AFROTC at Cornell University and reached the rank of 1st Lt. in the USAFRes.

Ron is married to Lois Anne McClure (Ithaca College 1955). They have three children - Suzanne Lynne Furry-Irish, married to L. Paul Irish; Kenneth B. Furry; and Donna Wynne Furry Tongue, married to Donald V. Tongue. The Furrys have five grandchildren and two great-grandchildren. His sister, Joanne Louise Furry, was married to his best friend and fellow Cornell classmate David P. Dirksen.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of historical documents relating to Ronald Furry's 42 years on the Cornell faculty in the teaching, research and Extension programs and the Department of Agricultural and Biological Engineering, including course material, research material and items on the Layer Fund, agricultural, biological, and environmental engineering.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ronald Bay Furry papers, #21-11-4261. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A variety of humorous quotations, jokes and cartoons. Includes floppy disk labeled "Boyd's Quotes!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Humorous quotations about Murphy's Law.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chinese proverb and calligraphy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of a joke test and one other humorous test.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explanation of origins of Cornell's Alma Mater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers presented to colloquiums, professional associations, agricultural extension bulletins, Farm Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo of Furry at Cornell Orchards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seminar, includes transparencies.

did.unittitle: Research pubs of agricultural historical interest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a variety agricultural bulletins and reprints of historic interest, eg, reprint of speech by Liberty Hyde Bailey, 1914.

did.unittitle: VHS Computer Magic: The World of Computer Imagery, Cinemagic Productions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: VHS, 58 Minutes, Produced by Colyer Dupont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo negatives, photo R. Furry with model of Pentairy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes B&W photos with close-up views of model Pentairy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Pentairy design notes, exhibit notes, newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes exhibit critique, additional Pentairy design notes, cow cartoon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page 21 photo of Plentairy exhibit in Cairo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sample of agriculture pubs with variety of dates. Includes sheet of slides from Oct. 1972 at Miner Institute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 3 sheets of slides of dairy related structures and 2 b&w photographs (size 8 x 11). Slides imprinted with the name W.W. Irish.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 sheets of dairy related slides at Miner Institute. A few with hand written labels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes floppy disk (unlabeled)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 3 pages of photos of classroom set-up and blackboard notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized by "week 1, week 2" etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research articles dated over a range of years.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes undated. Research articles from the mid-1960s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of whiteboard notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and research over a broad range of dates

did.unittitle: ABEN 465: G Pump listing, Pump Reservoir System
ABEN 465: G pump, Auto, Grapher Differ, Source & Execs, AutoCAD-Orchard.dwg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5" floppy disk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of deposition patterns

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research articles from 1966-1974, some annotated by Furry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published paper authored by Furry, handwritten notes and other research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Variety of materials including a USDA set of wheat samples, final exam for ABEN 465

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A variety of guides for slide rules.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Set of metal drawing instruments in a hard blue cover.

did.unittitle: Concise Conversion Tables, Circular Slide Rule

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plastic circular slide rule and paper instruction guide in a white plastic wallet, approx. 4" x 3"

did.unittitle: Concise Conversion Tables, Circular Slide Rule

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plastic circular slide rule and paper instruction guide in a blue plastic wallet, approx. 4" x 3"

did.unittitle: Book: "Fan Engineering", edited by Richard D. Madison, TJ960 F19 3rd ed.
